title: Published package exposes a build script that points to an omitted file
reasoning: The public package includes an npm `build` script that runs `node scripts/bundle-cli.mjs`, but the package `files` allowlist only publishes `dist`, docs, and release metadata. If a user or downstream release process runs `npm run build` from the installed tarball, the script target will be absent and the command will fail. This is not a runtime CLI failure because `bin` points at `dist/cli.cjs`, so severity is low.
reproduction: Pack and install the package, then run `npm run build` inside the installed package directory; Node will not find `scripts/bundle-cli.mjs` because it is not included by the package allowlist.
recommendation: Either include the needed `scripts` build file in the published package or avoid exposing a `build` script in the published package if rebuilding from the tarball is intentionally unsupported.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests or packaging smoke evidence are included for running package scripts from the produced tarball.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging smoke test that packs the CLI package, installs the tarball into a temporary prefix, and either verifies `npm run build` works or asserts the published package intentionally does not expose a rebuild script.
